results.possibleIdentity: Karl J. Widiner (also referred to in technical records as K. J. Widiner and in some indexes as Widinerz, K J.) was an engineer specializing in electro-mechanical design, active from at least the late 1960s through the 1980s. During the 1960s, he was associated with Varo Inc., specifically within the Electrokinetics Division located at 402 E. Gutierrez Street in Santa Barbara, California. His technical contributions include authoring or collaborating on technical reports for the U.S. Army Electronics Command, such as the 1967 report 'Development of Generator Direct Current G-63 (Hand Cranked)' and the 1968 report 'Research on the Flutter of Axial-Turbomachine Blading.' He is also listed as an inventor on a 1983 patent for a 'Method and apparatus for constructing slurry walls.' Widiner was associated with the organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), appearing in their technical membership records which matched engineers with artists for collaborative projects. He resided in the Santa Barbara area, specifically in Carpinteria and Goleta, and remained active in the local community through the early 2000s alongside his wife, Lynne Widiner.

webSearchQueries: "K. J. Widner" Experiments in Art and Technology
